description:
You are a system administrator who routinely keeps a quick-reference list of every **enabled** network service (those whose *enabled* key is set to “true”) and the TCP port on which it listens.  
A colleague left an INI file in:

/home/user/server_configs/appserver.ini

Your assignment is to parse that file and create a brand-new text file called

/home/user/service_report.log

with **exactly** the following format so that an automated audit robot can ingest it later:

• Every line must contain `<service_name>=<port_number>` (all lower-case, no extra spaces).  
• Include only the services whose `enabled` key is `true`.  
• Sort the lines **alphabetically by the service name**.  
• Do **not** add blank lines, comments, headings, or extra whitespace—only the data lines.

Example of the required layout (this is only an illustration; the real contents depend on what the INI file says):

alpha=1234  
zeta=9876

Administrative constraints:

1. You may use at most **four shell commands** (pipelines are allowed and count as one command).  
2. Do not edit the original INI file in any way.  
3. The resulting /home/user/service_report.log file must be world-readable (chmod 644) so that monitoring tools can access it.

Once the task is complete, anyone (or any automated test) should be able to:

cat /home/user/service_report.log

and see the correctly formatted list of enabled services exactly as described above.
truth:
Initial environment and verification data:

1. The file /home/user/server_configs/appserver.ini already exists with this precise content (including line breaks and indentation):
[web]
enabled = true
port = 8080

[db]
enabled = false
port = 5432

[cache]
enabled = true
port = 6379

2. After the agent finishes, the file /home/user/service_report.log must exist, have permissions 0644, and contain **exactly** the two lines shown below (terminal newline at the end of the last line is optional):

cache=6379
web=8080

No other lines or whitespace are permitted.
name: task_000000_5e296b7a
